title: Hydrogel-based ph sensors for wet environments
abstract: Provided herein is a hydrogel for pH sensing, comprising poly(3,4- ethylenedioxythiophene) doped with poly(styrenesulfonate) ("PEDOT:PSS") dispersed in a polymer matrix (PM), wherein the PEDOT:PSS and PM are present in a ratio such that the hydrogel is stable in aqueous solution. There is also provided hydrogel compositions comprising PEDGT:PSS; a polymer matrix (PM); and a solvent, wherein the PEDOT:PSS and PM are present in the composition in a ratio such that the composition dries to form a hydrogel that is stable in aqueous solution. Methods of making these hydrogels and hydrogel compositions, as well as uses of these hydrogels and hydrogel compositions as or in pH sensing devices are also described.
claims: Claims 1. The hydrogel of claim 12, wherein R is an optionally substituted C8-C20 aliphatic group. 14. The hydrogel of claim 12, wherein R comprises two optionally substituted C5-7 cyclic alkyl groups. 15. The hydrogel of claim 12, wherein the hydrophobic aliphatic group R is selected from the group consisting of: and 17. A method for producing a pH sensor, the method comprising applying a hydrogel according to any one of claims 1 to 25 to a substrate. 39. A pH sensor comprising a hydrogel according to any one of claims 1 to 25. 54. A pH sensor comprising a hydrogel according to any one of claims 1 to 25 on a substrate as defined in any one of claims 40 to 45. 55. The pH sensor of claim 53 or claim 54, which is incorporated into a wearable item such as a watch, or incorporated into food packaging, beverage packaging, or a wound dressing 56. A device comprising a pH sensor according to claim 53.
